forecast-period.text: A high pressure system south of Kangaroo Island will move slowly eastwards this weekend to be centred over the Tasman Sea on Monday. A trough is forecast to move across the western and central waters Tuesday afternoon and evening, pushing across southeastern coasts Wednesday morning. Another high pressure system is expected to become established south of the Bight later next week.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots in the west during the morning and early afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Partly cloudy.
North to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots in the west during the morning and early afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy.
North to northeasterly 15 to 25 knots turning west to northwesterly 15 to 20 knots during the afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres in the west.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon and evening.
East to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending north to northeasterly in the morning then tending east to northeasterly in the late afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres through the Backstairs Passage.
Partly cloudy.

Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending northerly in the early morning then turning east to northeasterly below 10 knots in the afternoon. Winds reaching up to 20 knots through the Backstairs Passage in the late morning.
Around 1 metre.
Southerly 1 to 1.5 metres through the Backstairs Passage, decreasing to around 1 metre during the morning.
Partly cloudy.
North to northeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning west to northwesterly 10 to 15 knots during the evening.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Mostly sunny day. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots west of Kangaroo Island during the morning and early afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots west of Kangaroo Island during the morning and early afternoon. Winds turning northerly in the early morning.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy.
North to northeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning west to northwesterly during the afternoon.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Becoming cloudy. 70% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon and evening.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: Easterly about 10 knots tending northeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the morning then turning east to southeasterly in the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Partly cloudy.
East to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending north to northeasterly before dawn then becoming variable about 10 knots in the evening.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly 15 to 20 knots during the morning.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore during the morning.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres inshore, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres offshore.
Sunny.
